hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Marketing and Sales Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. NAAC A+, NIRF #22. QS ranked. WES recognised.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Marketing and Sales Management suits professionals targeting brand management, digital marketing, or sales leadership roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 credential at Rs 1,99,000. The Sem 3-4 spec syllabus is confirmed each cycle — our counsellor shares it within 24 hours.

about.body:
Marketing and Sales Management is the second most searched MBA specialisation in India after Finance. The Amity Online MBA in Marketing and Sales Management builds capability across brand strategy, consumer behaviour, digital marketing, B2B and B2C sales management, and market research. The specialisation suits professionals in FMCG, retail, technology, e-commerce, media, and real estate who want to formalize and accelerate their marketing or sales careers.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with a NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025 and a QS World ranking. The Marketing MBA degree certificate carries the Amity University name identical to the campus degree. UGC-DEB entitlement means the degree is valid across private sector hiring and government notifications where online degrees are accepted.

EdifyEdu verifies these accreditation figures independently and does not receive commission from Amity. The programme runs over 2 years across 4 semesters. Semesters 1 and 2 are shared management core; Semesters 3 and 4 are the Marketing and Sales specialisation. See the full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amity MBA specialisations. See the main Amity MBA page for core subjects.
syllabus.emptyFallback:
Semesters 1 and 2 of the Amity Online MBA cover the management core: Accounting for Managers, Managerial Economics, Marketing Management, Statistics for Management, Professional Communication, Business Research Methods, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Legal Aspects of Business, and Conflict Resolution and Management.

Semesters 3 and 4 cover the Marketing and Sales Management specialisation subjects. These include areas such as consumer behaviour, brand management, digital marketing, integrated marketing communications, sales force management, retail and channel management, market research, and pricing strategy. The specific Sem 3 and Sem 4 subjects for Marketing and Sales Management are confirmed by Amity each admission cycle. Our counsellor can share the current spec-level syllabus within 24 hours. The final two semesters include a minor project in Sem 3 and a major project in Sem 4 applied to a marketing domain.

whoHires.body:
Marketing and Sales Management MBA graduates from Amity are hired across FMCG, technology, e-commerce, media, retail, and real estate. Entry-level roles (0-3 years experience) include: Marketing Executive, Brand Associate, Digital Marketing Analyst, Sales Executive, and Market Research Analyst. These positions are common at FMCG companies, digital agencies, e-commerce platforms, and D2C brands.

Mid-level roles (3-7 years) include: Brand Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Sales Manager, Product Marketing Manager, and Category Manager. Senior professionals with this credential reach titles such as VP Marketing, Chief Marketing Officer, and National Sales Head.

Key hiring organisations include: HUL, P&G, Nestle, ITC, Marico, Amazon, Flipkart, Swiggy, Zomato, Tata Group marketing units, Google, Meta, advertising agencies (Dentsu, Ogilvy), and real estate developers. The Amity Marketing MBA is recognised by FMCG and technology hiring managers who value the Amity NIRF and QS ranking signal in resume screening.

skills.body:
The Marketing and Sales Management specialisation develops skills that employers in FMCG, digital, and B2B sales test directly:

Brand Strategy: building brand positioning, identity frameworks, and portfolio management for consumer goods and service brands. Digital Marketing: SEO, performance marketing, social media strategy, Google Analytics, and email marketing — tools used daily in growth and marketing teams. Consumer Behaviour: understanding purchase decisions, segmentation, and behavioral economics for product design and campaign targeting. Sales Force Management: sales pipeline structuring, territory management, channel strategy, and key account management relevant to B2B and distribution-heavy sectors. Market Research: qualitative and quantitative methods, survey design, and data interpretation for launch and campaign decisions. Integrated Marketing Communications: coordinating advertising, PR, and digital touchpoints for consistent brand messaging.

Soft skills include persuasive storytelling, stakeholder negotiation, and performance metrics reporting — tested at Brand Manager and Sales Manager interview stages.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Marketing against peer programmes:

Amity vs LPU Marketing MBA (/universities/lovely-professional-university-online/mba): LPU offers a Digital Marketing specialisation separately. LPU Marketing is cheaper (around Rs 1,20,000) and NAAC A++. Amity's QS ranking and stronger brand recognition in corporate India give it an edge for FMCG and large technology company hiring. For pure digital roles, LPU Digital Marketing may offer more specific content.

Amity vs Chandigarh University Marketing MBA (/universities/chandigarh-university-online/mba): CU is NAAC A+ and NIRF #19. The fee is similar. CU has excellent placement infrastructure in north India manufacturing and service sectors. Amity's brand is stronger in national FMCG and media company hiring. Both are solid at this price point.

Amity vs Symbiosis Online Marketing MBA (/universities/symbiosis-university-online/mba): Symbiosis carries stronger brand recognition in Pune and western India. Fees are higher. For candidates targeting Mumbai and Pune consumer goods companies, Symbiosis brand premium exists. For Delhi NCR and pan-India digital roles, Amity is competitive.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA (all specialisations including Marketing and Sales Management) is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Additional charges may apply for examination fees. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the exact current fee at the official Amity Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Marketing and Sales Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For a sample certificate showing how the Marketing specialisation is recorded, request one via /contact — our counsellor responds within 24 hours.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Marketing alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Aditi Sharma
items.liked: Brand management module was taught with real HUL and P&G case studies
items.disliked: Sales force management needed more B2B tech sector examples
items.body: The brand management content was genuinely strong. Harvard-style case studies using actual FMCG brands made the theory stick. I moved from a marketing executive role to Brand Associate at a mid-size FMCG within 6 months of completing Year 1. The Amity name got me the interview; the case study knowledge got me the job.

items.name: Vikram Nair
items.liked: Digital marketing module updated with GA4, Meta Ads, and performance marketing
items.disliked: Market research module was too academic, needed more primary research practice
items.body: Digital marketing content was genuinely current — not 2018-era SEO theory. The GA4 and Meta Ads Manager labs were practical. I use these skills daily. Got a 35% hike moving to a Digital Marketing Manager role at an e-commerce company. Good ROI on the Rs 1.99 lakh investment.

items.name: Neha Gupta
items.liked: Consumer behaviour module helped directly in my FMCG brand research work
items.disliked: Placement support was generic — no FMCG or digital sector specific guidance
items.body: The consumer behaviour and brand strategy content directly improved my work quality in my current FMCG role. The degree helped me crack a lateral move into brand research at a larger company. Amity brand is well recognised in the FMCG sector — hiring managers know the name. Placement support was average; I found my own role through LinkedIn.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Marketing to Digital Marketing Management specialisation after enrolling?
faqs.answer: Specialisation changes are subject to Amity's policy and are typically not permitted after the first semester. If Digital Marketing is your primary goal, confirm at admission whether the Digital Marketing Management spec is available in your intake and enrol in that directly rather than Marketing and Sales.

faqs.question: What is the career difference between Marketing and Sales Management versus Digital Marketing Management at Amity?
faqs.answer: Marketing and Sales Management covers broader brand, sales, and channel strategy. Digital Marketing Management focuses on digital channels, analytics, and performance marketing. Marketing and Sales suits candidates targeting FMCG, retail, and B2B sales roles. Digital Marketing suits candidates targeting agency, e-commerce, or growth marketing roles.

faqs.question: Does Amity Online Marketing MBA have tie-ups with FMCG or advertising companies?
faqs.answer: Amity University has a placement network spanning 400+ companies including FMCG, media, and advertising firms. The online programme shares campus recruiter access. FMCG companies such as HUL, P&G, and ITC have historically recruited from Amity campus. Online MBA placement is assistance-based, not guaranteed.

faqs.question: What salary can I expect after Amity Online MBA Marketing?
faqs.answer: Fresh Marketing MBA graduates typically see packages in the Rs 4-7 lakh range. Professionals with 3-5 years of marketing experience commonly see 25-40% package jumps post-degree. Brand Manager and Digital Marketing Manager roles in mid-size companies are the most common lateral moves. Amity does not publish online MBA-specific salary data.

faqs.question: Is Marketing experience required to take this specialisation?
faqs.answer: No prior marketing experience is required. The Sem 1-2 core covers Marketing Management fundamentals applicable to any industry. However, working professionals with 2+ years of marketing, sales, or customer-facing experience will extract far more from the Sem 3-4 specialisation content. Freshers should build supplementary skills in Google Analytics and Meta Business Suite alongside the degree.

chooseIf: You are in FMCG, retail, or B2B sales and want a NIRF-ranked brand credential to qualify for Brand Manager or Sales Manager roles.
You are in Delhi NCR where Amity's corporate brand recognition is strongest for marketing hiring.
You want a broad Marketing and Sales credential covering both traditional brand and digital channels at under Rs 2 lakh.
consider: You are specifically targeting digital agency or performance marketing roles — the Amity Digital Marketing Management specialisation may be more directly relevant.
You are in Mumbai or Pune targeting FMCG or media companies — Symbiosis Online Marketing carries stronger regional brand premium.
You need specialised B2B or international marketing content — NMIMS Online or Manipal Online may offer more focused elective tracks.
